metadata.title: Broad catalogue of Indian consumer credit cards from official issuer surfaces
metadata.method: Manual normalization of names visible on issuer catalog pages, dynamic catalog tabs, official sitemaps, and issuer-owned product links. Competitor lists were not used to verify terms.
metadata.field_policy: null means unknown/not directly supported. annual_fee_inr appears only where the official catalog directly displayed it during this review. min_income_inr is omitted from every row because no card-specific minimum was directly supported in the inspected catalog evidence. status means catalog presence, not guaranteed application availability.
metadata.category_policy: Broad editorial discovery tags derived conservatively from the official card name and/or issuer catalog section; they are not contractual reward rules.
limitations: Catalog presence can include invite-only, relationship-only, upgrade-only, or stale legacy entries; re-check product pages before publication or recommendation.
Network is null unless explicit in the official name/catalog evidence or inherent to an American Express-issued product.
secured is null unless the official surface explicitly identified FD backing/secured status for that product.
Fees exclude GST unless the issuer catalog explicitly said otherwise; no fee waiver or income values are inferred.
Business/corporate cards were excluded even where issuer catalogs mixed them into consumer pages.

coverage.note: Main catalog was blocked by Cloudflare; official sitemap and official product URLs were used to establish listed product identities.

No current consumer credit-card catalog or product row could be verified on the accessible official DBS India surface; the legacy credit-card path redirected to a generic/error page. Zero rows rather than inventing a product.
OneCard is a technology/co-brand program with multiple partner-bank issuers shown on its official site; represented as one program-level product row, not duplicated by partner bank.
